stories.title: ‘Slap in the face’: Republicans skewer Pentagon over Poland move
stories.summary: Top U.S. Republicans on Friday condemned the Pentagon for canceling a U.S. troop deployment to Poland, an abrupt move that also appeared to catch Army leaders by surprise. The decision, House Armed Service Committee members said, amounted to a gut punch to the NATO ally and to a Congress that has sought to beef up the U.S. presence in Europe. They made those frustrations clear at a hearing with Army officials, where the service’s top civilian and uniform leaders had few answers about the rationa

stories.title: Ukraine ‘entirely justified’ to plan strikes against Russian energy and military targets after Kyiv attacks, Zelenskyy says – as it happpened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here Meanwhile, the Latvian president, Edgars Rinkēvičs, has begun political consultations aimed at finding a parliamentary majority capable of forming a new government after the collapse of prime minister Evika Siliņa’s administration. Rinkēvičs’s office posted a short video from the meeting on its social media. Continue reading...

stories.title: 13 men killed by US military boat strikes identified: ‘These were flesh-and-blood people’
stories.summary: All victims of US strikes in eastern Pacific and the Caribbean identified so far came from extremely poor communities Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A five-month investigation has named 13 previously unidentified victims of US attacks on boats allegedly carrying narcotics in a campaign that has killed nearly 200 people in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific. It is unclear if the US has ever identified any of its 194 victims before attacking them, and the names of just three

stories.title: ‘A watchdog without resources is not a watchdog’: Labor accused of letting key accountability body languish
stories.summary: Criticism of budget funding comes amid warnings the Australian National Audit Office will struggle to meet its targets for independent reviews Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has been accused inadequately funding one of the central institutions safeguarding public administration, with transparency advocates and independent David Pocock criticising commitments for the Australian National Audit Office (ANAO) in this week’s budget. Parliament’s Joint Committee of

stories.title: ‘An hour of abuse’: Jeremy Corbyn on Labour coups, and whether he feels sorry for Starmer
stories.summary: As Keir Starmer endures a slow ousting as PM, former Labour leader Corbyn recalls his own expulsion and looks at the runners and riders “Yeah, I do feel [sorry for him],” said Jeremy Corbyn, with only a little hesitation. “On a personal level it must be devastating. It is a horrible feeling. You suddenly realise that this person doesn’t trust you at all and really doesn’t wish you well at all, and you suddenly realise that any trust that was there actually disappears.” There are few in politics

stories.title: US stops Poland troop deployment after Germany pullout order
stories.summary: Both Democratic and Republican lawmakers in the US have criticized the Trump administration's plans to cancel a troop deployment to Poland. It came after Trump ordered the pullout of 5,000 troops from Germany.

stories.title: The Guardian view on life after Orbán: Péter Magyar’s fast start bodes well for Hungary and for Europe | Editorial
stories.summary: The new government in Budapest has already made an impact in Brussels. At home, the new prime minister is so far doing and saying the right things The transformative impact of Péter Magyar’s historic election victory over Viktor Orbán is already being felt in Brussels. On Monday, two days after Mr Magyar was sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, his new pro-EU government lifted the veto which for over a year has prevented the EU imposing sanctions on violent Israeli settlers. This followed a

stories.title: A Krausz Ferenc vezette Élvonal Alapítvány várja a párbeszédet Magyar Péterrel és a kormánnyal
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas kutató nevével fémjelzett alapítvány szerint a tevékenységük „teljes mértékben átfedésben vannak a Tisza-kormány által vállalt célkitűzésekkel”.

stories.title: Elronthatja az erős forint az idei balatoni szezont
stories.summary: A 10 százalékos forinterősödés nyomán többen dönthetnek a tengerparti nyaralás mellett, ami nem jó hír a balatoni szolgáltatók számára a felemás tavalyi szezon után.

stories.title: A BL-döntő, ahol a Manchester United cserejátékosai másfél perc alatt küldték a Bayernt a mennyből a pokolba
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére.

stories.title: Abszolút szimbólum: Árpád népétől az ÁVH irodaházán át a cigányság himnuszáig mindennek jelképes jelentése van Magyar Péter világában
stories.summary: Az új miniszterelnök láthatóan kedveli a kulturális jelképeket és gesztusokat, a székely és a cigány himnusztól Oláh Ibolya énekén át napokon belül jutottunk el a honfoglaló magyarság szentélyében tartott kihelyezett kormányülésig.

stories.title: Alig egy hete van ellenzékben, de már a sajtóért és a demokráciáért aggódik Orbán Viktor
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök szerint a hatalom el akarja hallgattatni a jobboldali nyilvánosságot, a Balásy-ügy is ezt a célt szolgálja. A Fideszből való kibeszéléseket azzal magyarázta, hogy a párt szellemi hátországát megviselte a hosszú kormányzás, az NKA-botrányból pedig egészen mást olvasott ki, mint a valóság.

stories.title: Herendi porcelánt küldtek Magyar Péternek, de szerinte ez nem az, amit Lázár Jánosék vittek el a minisztériumból
stories.summary: Több doboznyi porcelánt kapott a miniszterelnök ismeretlen feladótól, miután Lázár fejére olvasta, hogy hiányzik a porcelán a volt Építési és Közlekedési Minisztérium épületéből.

stories.title: Foszforeszkáló jelek a sötétben: olyan szemérmesre sikerült a Hárs-hegyi fényes ösvény, hogy alig találtuk meg
stories.summary: Két tucatnyi lelkes telexes és Telex-olvasó a múlt héten kísérletbe kezdett: az volt a terv, hogy éjszaka is látható jelekkel felfestik Budapest első éjszaka turistaútját.

stories.title: Azonnali hatállyal felmentették az Orbán-kormány közigazgatási államtitkárait
stories.summary: Nem maradhattak hivatalban.

stories.title: Az új kormányban már nem a külügyminiszter felel a magyar bor jó híréért
stories.summary: Komoly szemléletváltást jelez Magyar Péter kormányában, hogy melyik miniszter milyen területekért lett felelős. A legszembetűnőbb a rendészeti szemlélet visszaszorulása a szociálpolitikában és oktatásban, és az, hogy Vitézy Dávid egységben kezelheti a településfejlesztést a közlekedéssel. Na, de kihez kerül a bormarketing Szijjártó Pétertől?

stories.title: Roma children make history by performing Roma hymn at Hungarian parliament – video
stories.summary: Young, mostly Roma, members of the Sugo Tambura band realised their dream on Saturday by performing the Roma hymn at Hungary's parliament. The new prime minister, Péter Magyar, kept the promise he made to the children when he visited their village on the campaign trail. Roma rights campaigners have seized the moment, calling on the new government to ensure that the symbolism translates into real change Continue reading...

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.title: A meg nem választott vezető, akit Oroszországból támadtak, de most az USA buktatta meg
stories.summary: Bosznia működése és jogállama felett 1995 óta egy nemzetközi főképviselő őrködik. A német Christian Schmidt nem volt népszerű az országban, hatalmával élve könyékig turkált az ország jogszabályaiban. Ezzel nemcsak a szeparatista boszniai szerbek lábára lépett rá, hanem végül felbőszítette az oroszokat, amerikaiakat, sőt még egyes bosnyákokat is, szóval mennie kellett.

stories.title: Egy félrefordított és félreértett olasz leves, ami meghódítja a világot
stories.summary: Amerikában menyegzői leves néven lett népszerű, pedig nem lakodalmakra készült, csak a hús és zöldség összetartozásán alapszik. A receptje is egészen más, viszont meglepően rokon a mi lucskos káposztánkkal.

stories.title: A korábbi tervek ellenére mégsem telepítenek négyezer amerikai katonát Lengyelországba
stories.summary: A döntés azután született, hogy az Egyesült Államok nemrég bejelentette, ötezer katonát von ki Németországból.

stories.title: Török Gábor szerint nincs valódi ellenfele a Tiszának
stories.summary: A politológus szerint a 2026-os választás utáni magyar politika legfontosabb kérdése az, hogy lesz-e ilyen. És ha igen, akkor honnan fog jönni.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Helló, szia, szevasz! Van nálatok terasz?
stories.summary: Magyar Péter két miniszterével együtt lebontotta a Karmelitát több mint öt éve elzáró kordont, majd bejelentette, hogy Orbán Viktor volt hivatalát megnyitják a nyilvánosság előtt.

stories.title: Valter Attila szerint szinte jégpályává vált az út a magyar körverseny egyik lejtőjén
stories.summary: A Tour de Hongrie 3. szakaszán esőt és szelet is kaptak a bringások, a végjátékban nagy menés volt, de mindenki épségben teljesítette a szekszárdi dombokat. Merlier újra nyert.

stories.title: Szijjártóék még a választás előtti héten is kiosztottak 42 milliárdot egyedi kormánydöntésekkel
stories.summary: A választás előtti négy munkanapban 13 céggel írt alá támogatási szerződést a KKM, jutott a pénzből kínaiaknak, a Mercedesnek és a Hell logisztikai partnerének is.

stories.title: Betonba öntve találták meg a cseh női szent ellopott koponyáját
stories.summary: Nyolc évet is kaphat a férfi, aki a tervei szerint mély vízbe is dobta volna az ereklyét, hogy senki se találhassa meg.

stories.title: Június 1-jétől jön a Tisza Párt külföldimunkaerő-stopja. Milyen hatással lehet ez a magyarországi cégekre?
stories.summary: A Tisza-kormány júniustól befagyasztaná az új vendégmunkások behozatalát, a vállalatok viszont attól tartanak, hogy külföldi munkaerő nélkül beruházások és magyar munkahelyek is veszélybe kerülhetnek.

stories.title: Megrúgták Babért, egy vakvezető kutyát Budapesten, mert hozzáért egy szatyorhoz
stories.summary: Mivel nem sérült meg az állat, a gazdája nem tett feljelentést.

stories.title: Where is Dela Rosa? Philippine senator outmanoeuvres president in evading arrest
stories.summary: Chaotic week in which enforcer of ‘war on drugs’ flees senate building leaves government looking ‘incompetent’ The wanted man outran security agents , rallied protesters and even serenaded the media with a military hymn. Then, after a sudden exchange of gunfire, the Philippines’ most controversial lawmaker slipped out of the heavily guarded senate building in the middle of the night. Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the international criminal court for crimes against humanity, is now n

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy vows retribution over deadly Russian bombardments
stories.summary: President says three days of strikes will not ‘go unpunished’ and points to Ukrainian attack on Russian oil refinery. What we know on day 1,543 Volodymyr Zelenskyy promised retribution against Russia on Friday after laying red roses at the rubble of a Kyiv apartment building where a Russian missile strike killed 24 people , including three children. “Ukraine will not allow any of the aggressor’s strikes that take the lives of our people to go unpunished,” the president said after meeting top mil

stories.title: 1 in 3 Reform UK voters has a positive view of Tommy Robinson
stories.summary: LONDON — Tommy Robinson, the far-right anti-Islam activist who will lead a march through London on Saturday, is broadly unpopular in Britain — except among voters supporting Nigel Farage’s Reform UK.  According to The POLITICO Poll by Public First, which questioned more than 2,000 people earlier this month, 36 percent of Reform UK voters have a “very” or “somewhat” positive view of Robinson. About as many Reform supporters — 31 percent — had a negative view of him.  Robi

stories.title: Big promises, thin results from Trump’s China trip
stories.summary: BEIJING — U.S. President Donald Trump seemed very pleased with all that he and Chinese leader Xi Jinping discussed during his two-day trip. But how much they actually agreed to is unclear. During a Friday briefing with reporters on Air Force One en route back to the U.S. from Beijing, Trump revealed few substantive agreements while suggesting that at Xi’s behest he was rethinking a key element of U.S. relations with Taiwan. Trump provided no details of a potential U.S. sale of soybeans to China

stories.title: Pentagon quietly shut legally required program to prevent civilian deaths by military, watchdog finds
stories.summary: Trump administration accused of cutting military’s civilian harm program in light of US strike on girls school in Iran IThe Pentagon has quietly dismantled a program it is legally required to operate to prevent and respond to civilian deaths in US military operations, according to its internal watchdog. A report released by the department’s inspector general concluded the US military no longer has the people, tools or infrastructure needed to comply with two federal statutes requiring it to main

stories.title: Colorado governor commuted Tina Peters’ sentence after Trump blocked funding for clean water project, Lauren Boebert claims – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Colorado governor commutes sentence of election denier Tina Peters Sign up for the Breaking News US email Jamieson Greer also said US export controls on semiconductor chips were not a major topic of discussions with Chinese officials in Beijing . The US trade representative’s comments to Bloomberg on Friday suggest a breakthrough on selling Nvidia’s advanced H200 chips to China remains far away, Reuters is reporting, despite Nvidia chief Jensen Huang ’s last-minute

stories.title: Stateside with Kai and Carter: Stacey Abrams on why gutting of the US Voting Rights Act is ‘evil’ – podcast
stories.summary: The US supreme court dealt a devastating blow to the 1965 Voting Rights Act when it ruled in Louisiana v Callais in April that states cannot consider race in redistricting. Southern states from Tennessee to Alabama have rushed to erase majority-Black districts, sparking chaos for the midterm elections. Kai Wright talks to Stacey Abrams , a voting rights activist and former Georgia house minority leader, about the fallout from the decision, and why, despite it all, she still believes the way forw

stories.title: Teenager Isla Bell’s body was found in a Melbourne tip 18 months ago. Today friends and family demanded ‘justice’
stories.summary: Supporters at the Victorian state library protested against prosecutors dropping a manslaughter charge against the man accused of killing her Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Isla Bell, the 19-year-old whose body was found in a Melbourne tip 18 months ago, has been remembered as a loving, courageous and open-hearted young woman with a green thumb and an “exquisitely beautiful soul”. Friends, family and supporters gathered outside the Victorian state library on Saturda

stories.title: Mali’s forces target rebel alliance in junta’s fight to keep power
stories.summary: Army supported by Russian mercenaries launches airstrikes after offensive by coalition of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists Mali’s armed forces, supported by Russian mercenaries, have launched airstrikes targeting a rebel alliance of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists as the ruling junta struggles to maintain its hold on power in the unstable west African country. Earlier this week warplanes targeted the key northern town of Kidal, which was lost when the rebels launched a surpr

stories.title: Angus Taylor says migrants are a ‘net drain’ on Australia. The numbers say the opposite is true
stories.summary: Migrants are generally younger than the average Australian, are skilled and more educated. And they typically pay more tax than they receive in benefits Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Angus Taylor’s thinly veiled attempt to paint migrants as bludgers is not supported by the facts. The typical migrant over their lifetime pays more in taxes than they receive in government services. Far from lobbing up on our shores and demanding welfare , most migrants arrive hungry f

stories.title: Greece calls for EU action in Turkey fishing dispute
stories.summary: ATHENS — Greece demanded Friday that the EU “step in” over what it said was unlawful fishing and violations of maritime law by Turkish fishermen. “We ask the European Union to intervene,” Greece’s Shipping Minister Vasilis Kikilias said. “Our maritime borders are also European borders, and the law of the sea applies to everyone.” Kikilias raised the issue of “the provocative behaviour of our Turkish neighbors with regards to the unlawful fishing, the non

stories.title: Donald Trump does ‘not feel optimistic’ for Jimmy Lai after speaking with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Family and supporters had hoped the US president could help free the 78-year-old British citizen during summit talks in Beijing Donald Trump raised the case of jailed Hong Kong democracy campaigner Jimmy Lai in talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping but was told it “is a tough one”. Family and supporters of the 78-year-old British citizen had hoped the US president could help secure his release. Continue reading...

stories.title: FDA turmoil deepens as top drug chief departs claiming she was fired
stories.summary: Dr Tracy Beth Høeg, the Food and Drug Administration’s top drug regulator, says she was fired from agency after declining to resign In a major shake-up at the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA), top regulators left on Friday – including Tracy Beth Høeg, the acting drug chief, who says she was fired, and Katherine Szarama, the acting vaccines chief who has only been in the position for days. Jim Traficant, the chief of staff, has also been ousted. The FDA now has no permanent commissioner or d

stories.title: Trump says Islamic State ‘second in command’ killed by US and Nigerian forces
stories.summary: US president calls Abu-Bilal al-Minuki ‘most active terrorist in the world’ and says he was eliminated in ‘very complex mission’ Donald Trump has said US and Nigerian forces killed the “second in command” global leader of the Islamic State. “Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield,” the US president said on his Truth

stories.title: British Palestinians feel ‘gaslit’ and unable to speak out, says leading activist
stories.summary: Ahead of Nakba march, Sara Husseini says many feel they are being treated as suspects rather than victims of mass suffering British Palestinians feel unable to speak openly about Israel’s war on Gaza, the director of the British Palestinian Committee has said, amid what campaigners believe is a growing climate of hostility around Palestinian identity and activism in the UK. Some were afraid to wear Palestinian symbols at work or display Arabic jewellery and keffiyehs in public, Sara Husseini sai

stories.title: Lebanon says six killed in Israeli strike as US announces ceasefire extension
stories.summary: President Trump announced the truce last month, but Israel and Hezbollah have continued to exchange fire since then.

stories.title: Carney pitches Alberta pipeline pact as proof Canada still works
stories.summary: OTTAWA — Canada’s Prime Minister Mark Carney struck a pipeline agreement with oil-rich Alberta on Friday, presenting it as proof of a “Canada that works” amid a separatist strain in the province. Carney and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith met in Calgary to sign a deal that could eventually move more than 1 million barrels of Canadian oil a day to the Pacific, creating a direct export route to major energy markets in Japan, South Korea, China and India. The agreement marks a major shift in t

stories.title: US and Nigerian forces kill Islamic State’s ‘second in command,’ Trump says
stories.summary: American and Nigerian forces killed a leader of the Islamic State group in Nigeria in a joint operation carried out Friday, U.S. President Donald Trump said early Saturday. “Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, thought he could hide in Africa, but little did he know we had sources who kept us informed on what he was doing,” Trump said a social media post that offered few details. Al-Mainuki was viewed as the key figure in Islamic State organizi

stories.title: Trump noncommittal on Taiwan arms sales after meeting with Xi
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he was still deliberating a major arms sale to Taiwan, floating what would amount to a significant change in American foreign policy following a summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing. “I’ll make a determination over the next fairly short period. I’m gonna make a determination,” he told reporters of the potential sale as Air Force One made its way back to the U.S. from China on Friday, saying he needed to speak to the person “that’s running Taiwan.” The c

stories.title: Starmer drama has UK markets reliving their Truss nightmare
stories.summary: LONDON — Just as its Conservative predecessors did before it, Britain’s Labour government risks driving the U.K.’s economy into a ditch with an internal party fight over the steering wheel.  The pound has had its worst run against the dollar in 18 months, the stock market has slumped and the government’s borrowing costs have hit multi-year highs this week as Labour’s top brass turned on one another in response to a disastrous showing at nationwide elections last week. Unless it can re

stories.title: Labour tries to unite — so Farage can’t wreck their leadership fight
stories.summary: LONDON — Labour’s warring factions can at least agree on one thing: Nigel Farage risks throwing their plans to topple Keir Starmer into disarray. Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham looks to have emerged from an bitter internecine battle this week as the best contender to dislodge the deeply unpopular Labour prime minister from office. But to challenge the PM he must first win a by-election in northwest England that promises to be a political circus like no other.  Farage has vowed to “th

stories.title: UK joins European deal to send rejected asylum seekers to third-country hubs
stories.summary: All 46 Council of Europe members sign agreement ‘deplored’ by human rights organisations The UK and 45 other European countries have signed an agreement that explicitly endorses plans to send unwanted asylum seekers to third country hubs. A political declaration from the 46 members of the Council of Europe, the body that oversees the European convention on human rights (ECHR), said states had an “undeniable sovereign right” to control their borders. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump’s lack of focus on human rights in China is big departure for US diplomacy
stories.summary: Change reflects both transformation of US in Trump era and China’s increasing confidence on world stage Asked before he departed for Beijing if he would raise with the Chinese president the case of Jimmy Lai , the pro-democracy activist jailed in Hong Kong, Donald Trump said: “I’ll bring him up.” But, the US president added: “It’s like saying to me, ‘If Comey ever went to jail, would you let him out?’ It might be a hard one for me.” Trump was referring to James B Comey, a former FBI director and

stories.title: Supreme court rejects Virginia Democrats’ bid to restore congressional map
stories.summary: Order, issued without any noted dissent, is the latest twist in the nation’s mid-decade redistricting competition The supreme court on Friday rejected Virginia’s bid to restore a congressional map that would have given Democrats a chance to pick up four seats in the closely divided House of Representatives. The court’s order, issued without any noted dissent, is the latest twist in the nation’s mid-decade redistricting competition . It was kicked off last year by Donald Trump urging Republican-c

stories.title: POV: you’re Jim Chalmers using social media to sell the most ambitious budget of your life
stories.summary: Politicians still care about traditional media, but winning over people spending more of their lives online is the challenge for modern MPs Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast He’s up before sunrise , pounding the pavement in a cap and running shorts. He’s still up late into the night , having slipped into a comfortable sweater while he checks figures in a darkened office by lamplight, fuelled by sugar-free Red Bull. It’s “decision week”, Jim Chalmers declares of the ann

stories.title: Iraqi accused of terrorism attacks and plots in US and Europe arrested and charged
stories.summary: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood al-Saadi appeared in US federal court to face six terrorism-related charges The US justice department has arrested and charged an Iraqi national accused of involvement in nearly 20 alleged terror attacks and attempted attacks across the US and Europe . The wave of violence attributed to Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood al-Saadi has caused huge concern in many European countries but especially the UK, where Jewish community centres, charities, synagogues and other sites have

stories.title: US plan for Colorado River could cut up to 40% supply for Arizona, California and Nevada
stories.summary: Proposal comes after seven states drawing water from drought-stricken river failed to come to an agreement The US government has proposed a plan for the drought stricken Colorado River that could cut up to 40% of current supplies to Arizona, California and Nevada, as the waterway’s reservoirs continue to plunge to critically low levels. A top Arizona water official shared details of the Trump administration’s plan at a state meeting on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: I’m a Eurovision superfan, but this year’s contest brings only sadness. I won’t be tuning in
stories.summary: Mismanagement and political campaigning have sucked the joy out of an event meant to bring people together For the past two years, amid intensifying controversy over Israel’s participation in Eurovision, I and most other Eurovision superfans have stuck by the contest, despite clear misgivings. This week, however, as the usual collection of power ballads and jokey songs compete in Vienna, we are not bonding over a common joy, but rather over our shared sense of sadness about the politicisation of

stories.title: Stafford byelection tipped to swing towards LNP as danger looms for Steven Miles’ leadership of Queensland Labor
stories.summary: Voters in northern Brisbane electorate go to polls after sudden death of former independent MP Jimmy Sullivan Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A byelection in the Brisbane seat of Stafford is expected to swing towards the sitting LNP Queensland government in a result experts say would likely be fatal to former premier Steven Miles’ leadership of the Labor party. Voters in the northern Brisbane electorate of Stafford have gone to the polls after the sudden death of for

stories.title: Down and then out in Paris and London? Why Starmer isn’t the only one with a popularity problem
stories.summary: As continent faces tough headwinds, leaders are bearing brunt of delivering bad news to frustrated electorates “People hate you,” the adviser informed his leader. A think-piece in a daily newspaper noted that “almost everyone agrees on one thing: they don’t like him”. The recent disastrous set of local election results in the UK built on Keir Starmer’s longstanding reputational problem: only 11% of Britons believe he has been a good or great prime minister, and nearly 60% believe he has been poo

stories.title: UK drivers struggle to get insurance for Chinese EVs such as Jaecoo
stories.summary: Firms do not offer cover for some models, or charge more than for equivalent petrol cars, research finds UK insurers are more hesitant to cover some hybrid and electric vehicles (EVs) from China than cars from other countries, research suggests. While some drivers can save money by buying cars made in China, they may have more limited options to get insurance than those buying electric, hybrid and petrol cars from Europe, the US and South Korea. Continue reading...

stories.title: Record numbers of UK renters crowdfunding to cover bills
stories.summary: Rent donations on GoFundMe up 60% since 2022, with 100,000 donors helping people keep a roof over their heads A record number of people in the UK are turning to crowdfunding to cover rent and household bills, with GoFundMe reporting more rent-related fundraisers were created in April than in any month on record. The platform said donations towards rent support had risen by 60% since 2022, with more than 100,000 people a month contributing to help others meet their housing costs. Continue reading

stories.title: ‘Why are we even doing this?’ The week that left Britain’s PM looking like an interim leader
stories.summary: Week of leadership jostling has left Keir Starmer looking vulnerable and short of time – even though no challenger has officially come forward It was a minute or so into his BBC interview on Friday morning, after being asked about “moves” to remove Keir Starmer, that Steve Reed ran out of patience. “There is no contest,” he interrupted. “‘Moves’ mean nothing. People need 81 nominations to stand against the prime minister.” The housing secretary, a close ally of Starmer and a founding member of t

stories.title: Threatened indictment of Raúl Castro ratchets up US pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: Trump administration move echoes indictment of Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro as fuel crisis racks Cuba Tensions between Cuba and US seem set to rise further amid reports that Raúl Castro, the country’s 94-year-old former president, may soon face the type of indictment that led to the US abduction of the Venezuelan leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January. Although Raúl is officially retired, he remains the most potent figure in Cuban politics following the death of his brother Fidel in 2016, and by targe

stories.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo
stories.summary: Uganda also reports outbreak and health officials say cases were caused by Bundibugyo strain of virus An outbreak of Ebola has killed 65 people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health officials said. There have been 246 suspected cases of the haemorrhagic fever reported so far in the conflict-hit Ituri province, which shares borders with Uganda and South Sudan. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Russian drone attacks on Kyiv, Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Trump in Beijing and a mural of Lamine Yamal – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump says IS second in command killed in Nigeria
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, a high-ranking member of the "Islamic State," was killed in a joint mission between US and Nigerian forces.

stories.title: India news: PM Modi visits the Netherlands at start of Europe tour
stories.summary: Modi is looking to deepen India's trade agreements with its European partners and diversify beyond China-centric supply chains. Meanwhile, Indian farmers are once again clashing with the police. Follow DW.

stories.title: Australia: Man dies after shark attack off holiday island
stories.summary: A 38-year-old man has died after being bitten by a shark in waters off Rottnest Island in Western Australia. It is the second shark death this year amid a rise in attacks.

stories.title: Alleged murder of Aboriginal girl highlights Australia's deep inequalities
stories.summary: Her death has sparked outrage across Australia and intense debate about disparities in society.

stories.title: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran would be enough
stories.summary: The US president says Tehran has to show "real" commitment to removing nuclear fuel and stopping uranium enrichment.

stories.title: Five Italians die during cave scuba dive in Maldives
stories.summary: Four of the Italians were part of a team from the University of Genoa.

stories.title: Trump and Xi conclude 'very successful' talks but few deals confirmed
stories.summary: There were plenty of choreographed ceremonies but no trade breakthroughs after the two-day visit.

stories.title: Switzerland to open secret files on Auschwitz 'Angel of Death' Mengele
stories.summary: Mengele fled Europe after the war but for years there were rumours he spent time in Switzerland.

stories.title: What is a 'safe death'? Mentally ill woman asks for assisted dying in Canada
stories.summary: Canada has twice delayed access to medically assisted dying for those with mental illness. Claire Brosseau says she cannot wait any longer and wants the courts to decide.

stories.title: Palestinian leaders hold rare party meeting as polls show rising discontent
stories.summary: The Fatah faction elects top decision makers, but ordinary Palestinians feel the party and its leader are out of touch.

stories.title: From misfit to rap sensation: A 'Reble' storms into Indian hip-hop
stories.summary: Reble has emerged as one of hip-hop’s most distinctive new voices, channelling defiance into a sound rooted in India’s northeast.

stories.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein New York rape case
stories.summary: It marks the third time a New York jury has considered the case against the 74-year-old disgraced film mogul.

stories.title: US planning to criminally indict ex-Cuban leader Raúl Castro
stories.summary: Charges could come as soon as next week in a case reportedly focused on Cuba's downing of two planes in 1996.

stories.title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
stories.summary: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported - a case has also been reported in Uganda.

stories.title: A call to stop the global housing: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
stories.summary: More than 28,000 participants are set to gather in Baku for the UN's World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns that 3 billion people lack adequate housing and slum populations could triple by 2050.

stories.title: Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence, hours after summit with China's Xi
stories.summary: The US president says he wants Beijing and Taipei to "cool down" tensions over the self-governing island.

stories.title: Trump says senior IS leader killed by US and Nigerian forces
stories.summary: The US president says the joint operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

stories.title: Trump says 'most active terrorist in the world' killed in US-Nigerian operation
stories.summary: The "meticulously planned and very complex mission" eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the Islamic State group's second in command globally, Trump said.

stories.title: Man killed by 13ft great white shark in Western Australia
stories.summary: The man was bitten just before 10:00 (03:00 BST) at Horseshoe Reef - north-west of the popular Rottnest Island near the city of Perth, local police said.

stories.title: Death toll from large-scale Russian attack that slammed Ukraine rises to 24
stories.summary: The drone and missile barrage, that mostly targeted Kyiv, marked one of Russia's largest attacks in the past four years. Many EU leaders voiced support for Ukraine.

stories.title: Funeral in Jabalia after Israeli strike hits Gaza wake despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Residents of Jabalia bury two Palestinians killed in an Israeli strike on a wake, as Gaza reports nearly 73,000 dead and truce violations persist.

stories.title: Israeli army announces targeting of Hamas military chief in Gaza City airstrike
stories.summary: Israeli army said it targeted Hamas military chief in Gaza City air strike, meanwhile there has been no immediate confirmation from Hamas on the strike against Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

stories.title: UN pleads for Equatorial Guinea not to send US asylum seekers to their home countries: ‘Their life would be in danger’
stories.summary: Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the central African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries, where they face political violence, torture and death. The statement , co-signed by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s Rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial G

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports. In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy a

stories.title: After four bloody years, the war on Ukraine might be turning into Putin’s undoing | Rajan Menon
stories.summary: A battered economy, huge numbers of casualties and very little territorial gain – it’s no wonder even stalwart Putin supporters are showing signs of disquiet Rajan Menon is professor emeritus of international relations at Powell School, City University of New York On 9 May, Russia held its iconic annual Victory Day parade to honour the sacrifices of its soldiers and civilians during its four-year war against Nazi Germany. When the president, Vladimir Putin, invaded Ukraine on 24 February 2022, h

stories.title: German intelligence offices snub US-based Palantir software
stories.summary: Germany's domestic intelligence agency has reportedly chosen a data analysis system from France, instead of US-based Palantir. Civil society has welcomed the move, but says more must be done.

stories.title: North Korea slams UK over sanctions on camp allegedly linked to abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: Since the war in Ukraine erupted in 2022, 2,318 Ukrainian children remain missing, while 20,570 have been deported or forcibly displaced and 704 killed, according to a Ukrainian government portal.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Furnace Creek sizzles as snow sweeps Siberia in a week of extremes
stories.summary: Record heat in North and Central America coincides with egg-sized hailstones in eastern China Extreme weather across several parts of the world this week has brought record-breaking temperatures to Honduras, North America and Indonesia. Honduras smashed its all-time May maximum temperature record earlier this month – only for it to be broken again on 13 May in Choluteca, known as the furnace of Central America. Temperatures climbed to 42.2C (107.9F), surpassing the previous record of 42.1C. With

stories.title: UAE to complete second oil pipeline bypassing strait of Hormuz by 2027
stories.summary: State oil company fast-tracks previously undisclosed project, which is expected to double export capacity Business live – latest updates The United Arab Emirates has announced it will complete a new oil pipeline bypassing the strait of Hormuz by next year to secure its future crude exports against the threat of disruption. The current blockade of the vital waterway, through which 20% of oil and seaborne gas flowed before the Iran war , is approaching the 11-week mark, sending energy prices soari

stories.title: What was actually achieved at Trump and Xi’s ‘stalemate summit’ in Beijing?
stories.summary: US president has said he and Chinese leader ‘settled a lot of different problems’ but has given little detail on solutions Donald Trump’s whirlwind trip to Beijing – the first US presidential visit in nearly a decade – wrapped up with much fanfare but little clarity about what was actually achieved. Trump said on Friday he and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, “settled a lot of different problems that other people wouldn’t have been able to solve”. But he didn’t provide much detail on what those solut

stories.title: Oman caught between US and Iran after Tehran’s claims of joint strait of Hormuz plan
stories.summary: Muscat silent about plans – opposed by US – to charge fee and demand details on nationality of all transiting ships Oman has been caught in geopolitical crossfire after Iran said it was coordinating with the Gulf state over the future management of the strait of Hormuz, including Tehran’s plans to impose fees on commercial shipping. The Omani exclave of Musandam lies to the south of the contested waterway, which normally carries a fifth of the world’s seaborne oil traffic but has been blockaded

stories.title: German leader Merz says he ‘would not advise my children to go’ to US
stories.summary: Chancellor says he no longer views US as land of opportunity amid ‘deeply polarising’ social climate Friedrich Merz , the German chancellor, already embroiled in a row with Donald Trump over the Iran war, has said he would not advise his children to study or work in the US in the current climate. Speaking to a conference of young Catholics in Würzburg, the conservative leader, viewed by many as a transatlanticist, said he no longer saw the US as the land of opportunity. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Extremely cruel and tragic’: Iranian director Asghar Farhadi speaks out against state violence and the war
stories.summary: The film-maker, who won the Grand Prix for A Hero in 2021, condemned both the killing of protesters and the conflict’s bombing campaigns during a Cannes press conference Oscar-winning Iranian director Asghar Farhadi has described the deaths of civilians in Iran as “extremely cruel and tragic” during a press conference at the Cannes film festival. Farhadi, whose new Paris-set drama Parallel Tales premiered on the Croisette on Thursday night, was asked about working free from censorship in France,

stories.title: Shaky truce between Israel and Lebanon extended for 45 days, US says
stories.summary: Statement following ‘productive’ talks in Washington comes as Israel launches strikes on southern city of Tyre Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 45-day extension of their ceasefire after another round of talks in Washington, the US state department has said. It came after two “productive” days of talks, and more negotiations would be held from 2-3 June, the department spokesperson Tommy Pigott said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ebola outbreak confirmed in DR Congo, Uganda: African health agency
stories.summary: A new outbreak of the infectious Ebola virus has killed dozens of people in a remote part of Congo, according to the Africa CDC health authority. Neighboring Uganda later said one person had died in an "imported" case.

stories.title: India hikes petrol, diesel prices as economic woes from Iran war mount
stories.summary: For the first time in four years, Indian fuel retailers have increased the prices of petrol and diesel. Analysts warn this could be the first of many hikes.

stories.title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 — no need to worry about it hitting Earth
stories.summary: This big asteroid was discovered less than a fortnight before its flyby Earth. But any time you see a headline about asteroids flying by the planet, you can relax. Big and small asteroids fly by Earth all the time.

stories.title: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: Allegations of sexual abuse
stories.summary: The issue of systemic sexual abuses as part of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict is back in the spotlight following new testimonies. Both Israel and Hamas deny the claims.

stories.title: Uganda at a crossroads as Museveni begins new term
stories.summary: Unemployment and poverty pose major challenges for President Museveni's seventh term, while his age and long rule prompt questions about Uganda's future beyond his leadership.

stories.title: US-China summit exposes Trump's limits on Iran war, Taiwan
stories.summary: Donald Trump hopes to have China help him end the war in Iran, but this goal seemed far from guaranteed as he wrapped up his two-day visit to Beijing. Xi Jinping, in turn, took the chance to warn the US over Taiwan.

stories.title: UK politics: How Starmer survives as Farage waits in wings
stories.summary: High profile resignations, a prime minister on the edge and populists on the up — UK politics is far from dull. DW explains what's going on in Keir Starmer's Labour party and with Nigel Farage. And what may be next.

stories.title: Pakistan faces pressure over mediator role in Iran war
stories.summary: With Pakistan stepping in the middle of a major geopolitical conflict, Islamabad is forced to balance between Washington, Tehran, Beijing and key Gulf allies. A recent US media report places it under even more scrutiny.

stories.title: Ukraine: Dozens of European countries sign up to special tribunal plan to prosecute Russia
stories.summary: At talks in Moldova, more than 30 countries have said they will join a future special tribunal for Ukraine prosecuting Russia over its invasion. How the body will force Russians to stand trial is not clear.

stories.title: Islamic feminism more widespread in Bosnia and Herzegovina
stories.summary: Many Bosnian Muslim women want to attend mosque on Fridays and to play a greater role in decision-making. Slowly the official structures of the Islamic Community are changing.

stories.title: Germany news: Chancellor Merz says he wouldn't advise his kids to go to the US
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he wouldn't currently advise young Germans like his children to move to the US to study or work. He cited "the social climate that has suddenly developed."

stories.title: Middle East: Israel, Lebanon agree to extend truce, US says
stories.summary: The US says Israeli and Lebanese delegations have agreed to extend a ceasefire for 45 days. The truce has reduced the volume of fighting, but violations are commonplace, particularly in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Iraq swears in new prime minister as US presses Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups
stories.summary: Iraqi parliament approved Ali al-Zaidi's government on Thursday, with the new prime minister vowing a state monopoly on weapons as Washington leans on Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups.

stories.title: Underwhelming summit outcome in China brings Trump back to reality
stories.summary: After raising high expectations ahead of his trip to Beijing, the U.S president leaves with little to show for it, disappointing investors. On key flashpoints such as Iran and Taiwan, China didn't give any ground.

stories.title: Why is Ireland not taking part in this year's Eurovision?
stories.summary: Ireland's boycott is the latest in a series of moves that has seen the country at odds with Israel over its war in Gaza.

stories.title: Watch: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each in major exchange deal
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each in the first stage of a planned 1,000-for-1,000 exchange, mediated by the United Arab Emirates.

stories.title: Prisoner swap goes ahead as Kyiv mourns 24 killed in Russian strike on flats
stories.summary: Among the victims in Kyiv was 12-year-old Lyubava Yakovleva, whose father was killed during the war.

stories.title: Women's rights and demographics take centre stage at regional forum in Bukhara
stories.summary: Regional leaders and UN officials gathered in Bukhara to discuss how investing in women, education and demographic planning could shape long-term development across Asia.

stories.title: Watch: What did we learn from Trump's visit to China?
stories.summary: President Trump has concluded a two-day visit to Beijing at a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Five Italians die in Maldives cave diving tragedy
stories.summary: Rescue teams in the Maldives continued recovery operations after the country’s deadliest diving accident killed five Italian divers.

stories.title: AI vigilante trap snares alleged paedophile ex-teacher in France
stories.summary: The 66-year-old handed himself in after an influencer used AI to pose as a 14-year-old girl and broadcast their conversation online.

stories.title: What is the 'Thucydides Trap' that Xi invoked in his meeting with Trump?
stories.summary: Xi Jinping opened his Beijing summit with Trump by asking whether the two powers could avoid the "Thucydides Trap" — then warned that mishandling Taiwan could lead to war.

stories.title: New York Times defends journalist after Israel threatens to sue
stories.summary: Israel's prime minister says he has ordered legal action over an article which alleged Israeli security officials raped Palestinian detainees.

stories.title: CIA chief visits Cuba as energy crisis worsens
stories.summary: The reported visit to Havana came after the US renewed an offer of aid to ease the effects of its oil blockade.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend their ceasefire by 45 days following talks in the US
stories.summary: The US State Department will hold negotiations aimed at reaching a permanent political agreement on the 2 and 3 June. Beforehand, the Pentagon is to bring together military delegations from the countries concerned on 29 May.

stories.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas commander in Gaza air strike
stories.summary: Izz ad-Din al-Haddad, described as "one of the architects of the October 7 massacre", was killed in Gaza City, Israel says.

stories.title: What China critics in Maga movement make of Trump's Beijing trip
stories.summary: Trump’s softer approach towards China is one that experts say will likely trickle down to the wider MAGA base.

stories.title: Négy gólt kapott a Liverpool, de kettőt lőtt, mindkettőt Szoboszlai gólpasszával
stories.summary: Az Aston Villa már biztos ott lesz a következő Bajnokok Ligájában.

stories.title: London a 13. század óta bérleti díjat fizet az uralkodónak két olyan területért, amiről azt se tudja, hol vannak
stories.summary: Az idők során elveszett a pontos lokáció, de legalább a bérleti díj sem változott: két kés (egy éles és egy életlen), hat túlméretezett patkó és hatvanegy szög. Évente.

stories.title: Megnyitja Svájc a rettegett náci orvosról, Mengeléről szóló aktákat
stories.summary: Kiderülhet, hogy a Halál Angyal valóban járt-e Svájcban azután, hogy nemzetközi körözést adtak ki ellene.

stories.title: Az Egyesült Államok megölte az Iszlám Állam második emberét
stories.summary: Donald Trump közlése szerint az amerikai hadsereg a nigériai fegyveres erőkkel közösen végrehajtott támadásban ölte meg.

stories.title: A Balásy-korszak árát a rendezvényszervező iparágban is egy teljes tulajdonosi, vezetői és szakember-generáció fizette meg
stories.summary: A NER évei alatt teljesen leépültek azok az ágazatok, amikre rátelepedtek a Balásy-féle cégek. Így nézett ki belülről az állami piactorzítás, ami egy csomó vállalkozást tönkretett.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely szerint politikai bosszú a Krausz Ferenc vezette Élvonal Alapítvány 260 milliárdos szerződésének felmondása
stories.summary: Az ellenzéki Fidesz frakcióvezetője a döntés felülvizsgálatára szólított fel.

stories.title: Szájkarate: Aranykonvoj vs. amerikai kebelbarátság
stories.summary: Keressük a kampányidőszak legbizarrabb történetét. Melyik borított ki jobban minket: a lefoglalt ukrán pénzszállító vagy a dörgölőzés Trump holdudvarához?

stories.title: Az eső miatt lerövidítik a Tour de Hongrie királyszakaszát
stories.summary: A döntést a csapatok kérésére, a versenyzők biztonsága érdekében hozták meg.

stories.title: A német kancellár nem javasolná a gyerekeinek, hogy az Egyesült Államokba költözzenek
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz szerint mélyen aggasztó a társadalmi hangulat, ami kialakult az országban.

stories.title: Judge orders Trump administration to return Colombian woman deported to DRC back to the US
stories.summary: Judge called Adriana Maria Quiroz Zapata’s deportation to the Democratic Republic of Congo ‘likely illegal’ A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to bring a Colombian woman back to the US from the Democratic Republic of Congo , after she was deported to the African country that had refused to accept her. The deportation of Adriana María Quiroz Zapata “was likely illegal”, the US district judge Richard Leon ruled on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Ukraine corruption scandal reach Volodymyr Zelenskyy?
stories.summary: A drawn-out corruption affair in Ukraine has widened, with authorities now taking action against Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential office. Will the scandal reach all the way to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy?

stories.title: Foreign student fee hikes in France trigger backlash
stories.summary: A French government plan to charge students from outside the European Union 16 times more than previously has stoked discontent. But how does this compare to the rest of Europe, both inside the EU and out?

stories.title: Bolivia miners clash with police, President Paz under fire
stories.summary: Bolivian miners are calling for President Rodrigo Paz to resign, just six months after he was sworn into office. The Andean nation is going through its worst economic crisis in a generation.

stories.title: Marco Rubio says Cuba's leaders must go as US offers country €85 million in aid
stories.summary: Cuba lost the source for around half its fuel needs when US forces snatched Venezuela’s president in a stunning January raid, with his successor complying with US pressure not to aid the island.

stories.title: Seoul court orders North Korea's leader to pay compensation to Korean War POWs
stories.summary: Thursday's ruling was the third time former South Korean POWs have won damages against Kim and North Korea, according to local reports, although none has yet succeeded in collecting compensation.

stories.title: Peace activists rally to protect Palestinians during Jerusalem flag march
stories.summary: Israeli peace activists gathered in Jerusalem on Thursday to support Palestinian residents ahead of the annual Jerusalem Day flag march, an event often marked by nationalist tensions and heavy security.

stories.title: Anti-immigration AI videos traced to overseas fakers, BBC finds
stories.summary: "Patriotic" UK anti-immigration social media accounts have been traced to Sri Lanka and Vietnam.

stories.title: US reportedly seeks to indict Cuba's ex-president Raúl Castro as energy crisis deepens
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has renewed an offer of €85 million in aid on the condition that the assistance be distributed by the Catholic Church, bypassing the government.

stories.title: Protests erupt in Havana as Cuba faces severe fuel shortages
stories.summary: Small protests broke out in Havana over worsening shortages of food, power, and fuel, as Cuban authorities blamed US sanctions for the crisis and warned that fuel reserves had run out.

stories.title: Israeli nationalists chant ‘death to Arabs’ in violent Jerusalem Day march
stories.summary: Far-right Jewish marchers call for Palestinian villages to ‘burn’ as they storm through Muslim quarter of Old City Israeli nationalists chanted “death to the Arabs”, “may your villages burn” and “Gaza is a graveyard” in a state-sponsored march through Jerusalem to mark the anniversary of the city’s capture and annexation. The annual assertion of Jewish control over Palestinian East Jerusalem has grown more extreme in recent years, and Thursday’s event culminated with the national security minist

stories.title: Canada is welcome to join Eurovision, says song contest director
stories.summary: ‘We know that Mark Carney wants to sort of embrace Europe,’ says competition director Martin Green Canada is welcome to join Eurovision if it wishes, its director has said, months after the country revealed it wanted to “explore” joining the song contest in its federal budget. Eurovision director Martin Green told the BBC on Wednesday that Canada hadn’t yet applied, but would be welcome to. Continue reading...

stories.title: CIA director has met officials in Havana for talks, Cuba claims
stories.summary: Visit comes after US-Cuba relations deteriorated significantly, and as the island nation declared it had ‘absolutely no fuel’ because of US blockade CIA director John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday as a way to improve dialogue between the US and the communist-run island, the Cuban government said. The meeting took place “in a context marked by the complexity of bilateral relations, with the aim of contributing to the political dialogue between both nations”, a statement said

stories.title: Delta Goodrem Eclipse Eurovision second semi-final performance in full — video
stories.summary: Australia’s 2026 Eurovision hopeful Delta Goodrem delivers a powerful performance on the 70th anniversary of the global song contest, becoming the first Australian act to qualify for the grand final since 2023. Heading into the competition as an early favourite behind Eurovision heavy hitters Denmark and Finland, Goodrem delivers a note-perfect rendition of her power-ballad entry, Eclipse. Her performance marks the 11th time Australia has appeared in the multinational song contest Eurovision 202

stories.title: Felbontja a kormány a Krausz Ferenc vezette Élvonal Alapítvány 260 milliárdos szerződését
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas tudósnak a már kiutalt 22 milliárd forintot is vissza kell fizetnie.

stories.title: A 4iG 1311 milliárdos szerződése meglepte a befektetőket, de még egyes vezetőket is
stories.summary: A 4iG általában mindenféle tárgyalásról, szándéknyilatkozatról nagyon szívesen ad ki közleményt, arról viszont nem tájékoztatta a befektetőket, hogy a kapitalizációjánál kétszer akkora üzletre készül.

stories.title: Trump pekingi látogatása után visszavonhatja a Kínára kivetett szankciókat az iráni olaj vásárlása miatt
stories.summary: Kínának és az Egyesült Államoknak sem kívánatos az iráni helyzet, de nem tudni, mi lesz a folytatás.

stories.title: A Kutyapártnak el kell hagynia a székhelyét, most egy raktárba pakolnak
stories.summary: A visszafizetendő 640 millió forintból eddig nagyjából 145 milliót sikerült összegyűjteniük.

stories.title: Azt ígérte a Fidesz, bemutatják, hogyan zajlott az átadás-átvétel, de csak egy összevágott felvételt raktak ki
stories.summary: A Fidesz-frakció leleplezőnek szánt videójából túl sok konkrétum nem derült ki. A végén viszont felhasználtak egy másik felvételt, amin dossziék hevernek az utcán.

stories.title: Nem hoz megváltást a várható eső, és a gazdák szkeptikusak, hogy mit tud elérni a kormány
stories.summary: A Mezőgazdasági Szövetkezők és Termelők Országos Szövetségének főtitkára rövid távon nem bizakodó, szerinte a gazdatársadalom egyelőre kivár, hogy mi valósul meg a kormány bejelentései közül.

stories.title: Harminc év alatt háromszor kapta meg az utolsó vacsorát, most elhagyhatta a börtönt
stories.summary: Az amerikai Richard Glossip egy 1997-es gyilkosság miatt került rács mögé, kilenc időpontja volt a kivégzésére.

stories.title: Viccből küldött ismerősének hamis katonai behívót egy férfi Dömösön
stories.summary: A rendőrség felgöngyölítette az ügyet.

stories.title: Visszaköltözik a Pénzügyminisztérium a József nádor térre
stories.summary: Kármán András pénzügyminiszter szerint az épület „már éppen nem esett áldozatául az értékes állami műemléképület-mutyinak”.

stories.title: Újabb államtitkárt mutatott be Gajdos László: Kőrösi Levente felel majd a természetvédelemért és az állatjólétért
stories.summary: Az élő környezetért felelős miniszter korábban már két másik emberét is bemutatta.

stories.title: Ellentétben Kövér Lászlóval, Forsthoffer Ágnes nem Tisza István székén ülve fog dolgozni
stories.summary: Kiderült, hogy az Országgyűlés korábban a Nemzeti Múzeumtól szerezte meg Tisza István székét, amiben 2013 és 2026 között vezette az üléseket Kövér László. Az új házelnök gondoskodik arról, hogy kiállítsák a széket.

stories.title: Meghalt Lazare, a világ legidősebbnek tartott kutyája
stories.summary: A francia kutya első gazdáját is túlélte, de az újnál csak néhány hetet töltött.

stories.title: 205-205 hadifoglyot adott át egymásnak Oroszország és Ukrajna
stories.summary: Volodimir Zelenszkij ukrán elnök szerint ez csak az első lépése volt egy nagyobb fogolycserének. Korábban mindkét fél egyenként 1000 fogoly cseréjéről állapodott meg.

stories.title: Kilépett a Fideszből Balassagyarmat polgármestere
stories.summary: A kilépést személyi és szervezeti okokkal indokolta, amelyek miatt szkeptikusan áll a Fidesz megújulásához.

stories.title: Mítosz, hogy szülés után hat héttel már bármit lehet csinálni
stories.summary: Nagyon kell vigyázni az intimtornával, mert ha valakinek nincsen testtudata, akár árthat is vele. A szülés utáni hathetes kontrollon megtévesztő eredmények születhetnek, mégis nagyon fontos – mondták a meghívott szakértők a Tabu című műsor első adásában.

stories.title: Tizennyolc év után megszűnhet a SzegedMa
stories.summary: A Mediaworks által kiadott, kezdetektől fogva jobboldali portál első születésnapjára még Lévai Anikó is levelet küldött, és lelkes olvasóként hivatkozott magára. A Szegeder információi szerint felmondtak a munkatársaknak.

stories.title: Kórustalálkozóra tartó iskolások busza égett ki az M0-son
stories.summary: A tűzben senki sem sérült meg, de a buszon maradt holmijuknak annyi.

stories.title: Találtunk még pár százmillió forintot, amit kiosztottak Mága Zoltánéknak
stories.summary: Nem csak az NKA és a KIM szórta a százmilliókat Mága Zoltánnak és körének, az EMMI idején is kifejezetten jól járt a Fidesz prímása.

stories.title: Lázárék döntése értelmében saját tulajdonból bérelt irodába költözik a MÁV több mint 500 dolgozója
stories.summary: A XIII. kerületi Metropol Irodaházba költözik a MÁV több részlege.

stories.title: Szécsi Zoltán a személyét ért támadások miatt távozik az Egri Vízilabda Klub éléről
stories.summary: A háromszoros olimpiai bajnok vízilabdázó nyolc évig volt a klub elnöke. Az áprilisi választások után egy ideig úgy volt, a Fidesz-frakció tagja lesz a parlamentben.

stories.title: Meghalt Sörös Sándor színész
stories.summary: Tizenhat évig volt a Vígszínház tagja, de ő szinkronizálta Tom Sellecket a Magnumban. 74 éves volt.

stories.title: A férj azt állítja önvédelemből ölte meg a modell feleségét, de azt, amit a holttesttel tett, már nem tudja tagadni
stories.summary: Kristina Joksimovicot 2024. februárjában ölte meg a férje. A nő maradványait brutális módon próbálta meg eltüntetni, de a bíróságon most azt állítja, hogy önvédelemből cselekedett, és bánja a tetteit. A pszichiáter szakértő és a gyerekek képviselője viszont cáfolja az állításait.

stories.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
stories.summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

stories.title: Honda reports first loss since 1957 as it waters down EV strategy, but shares rise on 2026 forecasts
stories.summary: The Japanese carmaker pursued an aggressive switch towards electric vehicles that it's now trying to walk back. Despite posting its first loss since 1957, its share price spiked on more buoyant predictions for 2026.

stories.title: Who is Mario Draghi, this year's Charlemagne Prize winner?
stories.summary: The Charlemagne Prize honors those who advance European unity. This year's winner is Italy's Mario Draghi, a former European Central Bank chief and a key architect of European stability during a time of economic crisis.

stories.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather: More chicken than dinosaur?
stories.summary: A handbag is promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product." But scientists are questioning its authenticity.

stories.title: UK: Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigns, likely in bid to topple PM Keir Starmer
stories.summary: Two Labour Party politicians have positioned themselves to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership. Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned, and Angela Rayner said her tax affairs were back in order.

stories.title: UAE denies Netanyahu office's claim of secret wartime visit as Iran dubs it 'unforgivable'
stories.summary: The UAE denied Netanyahu's office's claim of a secret wartime visit, as Tehran threatened those "colluding with Israel".

stories.title: Ship seized near UAE coast headed for Iranian waters, UK maritime agency says
stories.summary: A ship has been taken near the UAE coast and is headed for Iranian waters, the UK's UKMTO said, in the latest vessel seizure around the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Elon Musk brings son X Æ A-Xii to high-level business meeting with China’s premier
stories.summary: Top US business leaders including Elon Musk, Tim Cook and Jensen Huang met Chinese Premier Li Qiang in Beijing on Thursday during high-level China-US business talks held alongside President Trump’s visit, with Musk seen arriving alongside his son X Æ A-Xii.

stories.title: Trump and Xi toast each other at Beijing banquet
stories.summary: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping toasted each other during a state banquet in Beijing attended by executives including Elon Musk, Tim Cook and Jensen Huang following high-level China-US talks, where Trump also invited Xi to visit the White House in September.

stories.title: "Super Mario" celebrated for defending the euro and European unity
stories.summary: Former European Central Bank president Mario Draghi received the 2026 Charlemagne Prize on Thursday, 14 May, in Aachen, Germany, in recognition of his contributions to European unity and his leadership during the eurozone debt crisis of the 2010s.

stories.title: Warning of record global temperatures
stories.summary: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows

stories.title: Hundreds of illegal motorbikes bulldozed in New York City crime crackdown
stories.summary: A moped had been used in the deadly stray-bullet shooting of a 7-month-old in Brooklyn last month, officials said.

stories.title: Erdoğan visits Astana as Turkey and Central Asia deepen ties amid shifting regional order
stories.summary: Turkey and Kazakhstan signed a friendship and strategic partnership declaration in Astana, targeting €13 billion in trade.

stories.title: Trump and Xi wrap up summit claiming progress in stabilising ties but differences remain
stories.summary: The two leaders met on Friday at final talks of the superpower summit, which the US president said "settled a lot of different problems". Xi described Trump's visit as a "milestone visit".

stories.title: Exclusive: Inside Gaza's hunger crisis as aid falters and funding dries up
stories.summary: Community kitchens serving basic foodstuffs like lentils once a week are the last food source for hundreds of thousands in Gaza, as aid flows have dropped by 37% and only 10% of funding has been secured, Euronews learns on the ground in the Strip.

stories.title: Iran eyes fees on Hormuz internet cables as new lever against the West
stories.summary: IRGC-linked media have called on Tehran to impose fees on submarine cables through the Strait of Hormuz and monitor global data traffic, citing claimed sovereignty over the waterway.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon meet for third round of direct talks in Washington
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon will meet for a second day in Washington to continue its third round of direct talks, as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues.

stories.title: Xi gives Trump private tour of secret garden in Beijing
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping accompanies US counterpart Donald Trump on a walk through the gardens of Zhongnanhai, a central leadership compound next to Beijing's Forbidden City.

stories.title: King Charles and Idris Elba share the DJ stage at royal garden party
stories.summary: King Charles III marks 50 years of The King’s Trust at Buckingham Palace, celebrating its support for 1.5 million disadvantaged young people worldwide.

stories.title: For three years I scoured the world for answers to Europe’s big problems – here’s what I found
stories.summary: Japan and Taiwan have enviable care systems because they had the courage to plan – some solutions are radical, most are hiding in plain sight It’s mid-afternoon in Fujisawa. Schoolchildren, rucksacks on their backs, bound into a room where a group of pensioners welcome them boisterously, before sitting them down to help with their homework. This group of older people is looked after by some of the pupils’ parents. Up the road, a cluster of university students live above some over-75s. They get h

stories.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
stories.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.

stories.title: US drops bond requirement for FIFA World Cup ticket holders
stories.summary: Fans from five African World Cup qualifying nations with valid tickets are exempt from paying a hefty bond. But fans from several other countries still face major hurdles to seeing their teams play in the United States.

stories.title: Syria needs 'comprehensive security agreement' with Israel, foreign minister tells Euronews
stories.summary: Al-Shaibani calls for a return to the 1974 security agreement between Israel and Syria and demands the withdrawal from territories seized. Remarks coincide with high-level political dialogue between Damascus and Brussels.

stories.title: Europe watches Beijing summit from the sidelines and fears the worst
stories.summary: The highly anticipated summit between US President Trump and his Chinese host Xi Jinping has begun - and Europe is watching from a distance. Yet, whatever the outcome is, there is little Brussels can be optimistic about.

stories.title: Princess Catherine visits Italy on first overseas trip since cancer diagnosis
stories.summary: Britain’s Catherine, Princess of Wales visited the Italian city of Reggio Emilia during her first official overseas trip since announcing her cancer remission.

stories.title: Trump arrives in Bejing for crucial summit, welcomed by Vice President Han
stories.summary: The US president said he will be talking to his Chinese counterpart about trade “more than anything else.”

stories.title: 250,000 pilgrims gather at Fatima shrine in Portugal for annual candlelight Mass
stories.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Catholic pilgrims gathered at Portugal’s Fatima shrine for the annual Mass of the Blessing of the Candles honouring the Virgin Mary.
